kept.text:
非常认同作者的看法，GPT-5.5彻底反转局面，而Opus 4.7缺乏4.6的惊艳，出现不说人话、长上下文成功率暴跌等问题。背后原因是Anthropic算力短缺，相反OpenAI算力领先

我的意见... 至于下一阶段谁能解决推论算力问题... 就是throughput...

别说我偏袒 $AMD 了， 我从来就是说 $NVDA 才是王者

kept.text:
$AMD $META Dr. Su comment🤝

"We actually see an opportunity to go much broader than that in the sense that you're actually charting the path for where AI infrastructure is going in the future.  So the value that's accrued to AMD of a deal like this is, yes, we get to accelerate purchases, which is a great thing, but we also get to accelerate our technology ecosystem, our software ecosystem that accrues benefits beyond just the work that we're doing with Meta, but really to the overall AMD ecosystem.  And the key with how we've designed these warrants is they're very, very performance-based.  So in some sense, both companies are incented to help each other win.  We win when Meta's foundational models are super successful and they need lots and lots of chips, so we are motivated to give them the best infrastructure for their workloads.  And they're motivated to ensure that our ecosystem is as strong as it can be.  And there's a very good win-win synergistic partnership.  But it is a special thing.  I think the conversation is around we want to build a very rich ecosystem."

quoted_tweet.text:
$AMD 🤝 $META to x3 from here 🧵
Not Financial Advice!

The 10 analysts downgraded $META today are as clueless as those that downgraded @AMD in 2023.

They have no idea what they downgraded. 

AMD's partnership with Meta directly supports accelerated AI infrastructure scaling, cost efficiency, and faster model deployment key drivers for Meta's long-term growth. Especially Helios Rack will bring down Inference cost to $0.0003-$0.0005/M Tokens. Read linked threads below to understand more.

1. Why Analysts are wrong?

Q1 2026 results crushed estimates: Revenue $56.3B (+33% YoY), adjusted EPS beat handily (strong net income growth even excluding tax benefits). Ad impressions and pricing powered growth.  33%. Read it again 33% at this size!!!!

- 19% YoY jump in ad impressions
- 12% YoY jump in avg price per ad
- 29% FXN YoY revenue growth

Fwd P/E multiple should be 27-30x. not 20-21x 
at this kind of growth for this size!

Meta is projected to surpass Google in global digital ad revenue in 2026 (~$243B vs. ~$240B), with faster growth (24%+ vs. Google's ~12%). AI tools like Advantage+ (end-to-end automated campaigns), Reels monetization, and generative creative features are driving higher engagement, better ROI for advertisers, and pricing power.

AI-powered optimizations have boosted clicks, conversions, and efficiency. Advantage+ and related tools have a massive revenue run-rate (previously cited in the $60B+ range in prior quarters). This isn't speculative, it's fueling the ad machine that makes up ~97% of revenue.

Mark Zuckerberg and management express high conviction in the spend, pointing to industry signals, custom chips AMD, efficiency gains, and building toward leading models/products. They expect operating income to still grow in 2026 despite higher expenses

2. How Inference cost to $0.0003-$0.0005/M Tokens Helps Meta Accelerate Growth

Cheaper AI everywhere = more usage & revenue: Meta’s ad business (~97% of revenue) runs on AI recommendations, creative tools (Advantage+), and Reels. Ultra-low inference costs let Meta run far more AI queries, personalize better, generate more content, and improve advertiser ROI driving higher engagement, ad impressions, pricing power, and growth (already +33% revenue in Q1 2026). 

Mark Zuckerberg’s vision for advanced AI features across 3.5B+ users becomes economically viable. Low costs support massive deployment without destroying margins, even as capex rises to $125–145B in 2026.
Efficient inference helps operating income grow despite higher spend.

A full Helios rack packs up to 72 custom MI450-based Instinct GPUs + 6th-gen EPYC "Venice" CPUs. It delivers massive scale-out performance (1.4 exaFLOPS FP8, up to 2.9 exaFLOPS FP4) with 31 TB+ HBM4 memory and 1.4 PB/s bandwidth per rack. This enables huge batch sizes, higher parallelism, and better utilization for inference-heavy workloads lowering cost per token vs. smaller or less integrated systems

The MI450 variant is tuned specifically for Meta’s workloads (heavy on inference, ranking/recommendations). Paired with Venice EPYC CPUs (co-optimized by Meta), it boosts tokens/second while reducing power draw and overhead. Open Compute Project  Open Rack Wide design improves cooling, networking, and serviceability, cutting TCO further. 

Conclusion: 

The long term partnership between Dr. Lisa Su and Mark Zuckerberg is a powerful catalyst accelerating Meta’s AI infrastructure advantage, powering sustainable revenue growth of 30%+potentially hitting 35%+ in key periods well ahead of most other mega-cap peers. 

 Dr. Su emphasized: “This multi-year, multi-generation collaboration... aligns our roadmaps to deliver high-performance, energy-efficient infrastructure optimized for Meta’s workloads, accelerating one of the industry’s largest AI deployments.” Zuckerberg responded: “We’re excited to form a long-term partnership with AMD to deploy efficient inference compute and deliver personal superintelligence... I expect AMD to be an important partner for many years to come.” 

Meta’s Q1 2026 results already showed +33% revenue growth (crushing estimates), with strong momentum into Q2 guidance. This outpaces typical mega-cap peers: Google/Alphabet’s ad growth in the mid-teens, slower overall revenue ramps at many others amid AI investment pressures. The leaderships alignment positions Meta to sustain or exceed 30–35%+ topline expansion as AI monetization compounds faster than most peers burdened by higher competition or less direct ad-AI flywheels. 

Analyst downgrades obsess over near-term capex optics and margin noise, but they miss the execution flywheel. The proven ad business + efficient AI scaling via partners like AMD is converting massive investment into durable competitive advantage and superior growth. History favors Meta’s big bets when they focus on user/ad value this one looks set to deliver another powerful rebound and compounding returns. The current post-earnings dip could be a compelling entry for long-term believers in Meta’s AI-augmented advertising dominance.

Not Financial Advice!
